timestamp : 2026-04-10T21:28:24+02:00

input     : Candidate: The GIL is a mutex in CPython that allows only one thread to execute bytecode at a time, which prevents true parallelism in CPU-bound threads. I'd use multiprocessing for CPU-bound work instead.
output    : A:The GIL is a mutex in CPython that allows only one thread to execute bytecode at a time, which prevents true parallelism in CPU-bound threads. I'd use multiprocessing for CPU-bound work instead.A:The GIL is a mutex in CPython that allows only one thread to execute bytecode at a time, which prevents true parallelism in CPU-bound threads. I'd use multiprocessing for CPU-bound work instead.
